title: MATH1241 BT 2025 week1
body:
UNSW Sydney 2025 Term 2 MATH1241 Higher Mathematics 1B Tutorial week 1

Themes: What we learnt from your students surveys Algebra: Revision of linear system of equations in preparation for MATH1241 Calculus: Revision of limits and differentiation in preparation for MATH1241

[ -1] This first thing your tutor will do is discuss what we learnt from your myExperience surveys.

[0] • Find a group and some board space. • Grab a whiteboard marker. Each group member should have a different colour. • Introduce yourself to your group members. Some ideas: your name, what you are studying and why you chose it, an interesting fact about yourself. • Write your name (First name + last name) at the top of the board.

And now let the maths start! NB: Do not worry if this tutorial takes place before your first lecture: It only requires MATH1141 knowledge.

[1] Unusual looking equation of a plane. Consider the equation   x−1 y−2 z+1 det  1 0 2  = 0. 2 −1 0

(a) Show that the equation represents a plane Π and give an equation of Π in parametric form. (b) Write an equation of the plane Π in point-normal form.

[2] Limits, derivatives and functions defined by an integral. Using the following Maple session, or otherwise, answer the questions below. > int(exp(-t^2), t=0..infinity); √ π Rx 2 (a) Let F : R → R defined by F (x) = arctan(x) 0 e−t dt. (i) Find, if it exists, lim F (x). x→∞ (ii) Find F ′ (x) and then, if it exists, lim F ′ (x). x→∞   2 2 (b) Find an antiderivative of g : R → R defined by g(x) = x17 −2xe−x + 17x16 e−x . Z x  −x2 4x3 2 (c) Find an antiderivative of h defined by h(x) = ln 1 + x e4 + 4 e−t dt. 1+x 0 (d) Let f, g be real-valued functions defined on R with f continuous Rx on R and g differentiable Rx on R. Find an antiderivative of φ defined by φ(x) = g ′ (x)e 0 f (t)dt + g(x)f (x)e 0 f (t)dt .  [3] Linear system of equations with parameters Let α, β ∈ R. Consider a system of linear equations with augmented matrix   α β 2β β 0 α + 1 1 3 . 0 0 α +α α

For all values of the parameters α and β, determine the number of solutions and describe them geometrically. For instance your answer could contain statements of the form; “If β = [a number], for all real numbers α, the solutions form a line through the point A [give its coordinates] whose direction is given by the vector → − v [give its entries].
